items.title: 0 Button Untamed Power 30k
items.rotation: Set your pet skills on auto cast (ctrl right click).
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity ranger build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:35 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript: Welcome to my zero-button build. You have made it all the way to the bottom. There's not really much to say about the build. The only thing you have to make sure of is that you set your pet skills on autocast. Control-right-click the pet skill to set it to autocast, and that way the pet will do its own thing and you don't have to do anything. You just have to press one once. For useful skills, Sword 2 is a big leap and Sword 3 is a big leap, so there is a lot of mobility on this build. Axe 4 is a CC. You can pretty much ignore Axe 5, but it's technically a reflect. If you unleash your pet, you get two useful skills: F3, which is a projectile block, and F2, which is a boon strip ability. But do note that if you unleash your pet, your pet skills are no longer on autocast, so make sure you go back so your pet can keep playing the game. You get projectile reflect, projectile destruction, decent mobility, and a little bit of CC. For utility skills, Healing Spring is pretty good. It's condition removal for a healing skill. Elite Entangle is useful. It provides immobilize for a very long time. For the most part, the rest of the ranger utility skills aren't that great. You have a little bit of CC from Untamed. Protect Me! is a group stun break. Search and Rescue! will revive a downed ally, so it's nice if your team gets downed a lot. Signet of Stone is situationally useful because it gives you invulnerability, but be careful because a lot of raid wings have toughness-based tanking, so if you run it then you may end up tanking the boss. That's about it. It's mostly damage and other selfish utility, but you already have a decent amount built into your kit with Axe 5, Axe 4, and your pet skills. Thank you for watching, and I hope you enjoy the zero-button build.

items.title: 3 Button Untamed Power 33.9k
items.rotation:
Opener is Frost Trap into Hammer 4 into Hammer 2.

Rotation priority order is Hammer 2, Hammer 4, Frost Trap.

Set your pet skills on auto cast (ctrl right click).
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity ranger build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:19 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, your opener is Frost Trap into Hammer 4 into Hammer 2. Your priority order during the rotation itself is the opposite: Hammer 2 into Hammer 4 into Frost Trap. Damage-wise, Hammer 4 is very similar to Hammer 2, so during the rotation it doesn't really matter which one you use first. The only reason the opener is Frost Trap into Hammer 4 first is that Frost Trap and Hammer 4 have a wind-up time. Frost Trap takes basically a second to proc after you cast it, and while it takes that second, you're effectively fitting in the Hammer 4 cast time during it. It's a slight min-max.

You don't want to cancel your auto-attack chains. Your auto-attack chains are important, so make sure that you get your third hit off. You want to fit in your Hammer 2 and Hammer 4 in between those big slams. It'll be a slight damage decrease if you end up canceling your auto-attack chain, but it's not a big deal. You'll still probably be doing about 30k DPS.

For notable skills, if you go into Unleash Pet, your F2 is a boon strip and your F3 is a projectile destruction skill. Your Hammer 3 and Hammer 5 in Unleash Pet are CC skills. Be careful because while you're in Unleash Ranger form, your Hammer 3 and Hammer 5 don't CC. So if you're expecting to take down a break bar, Unleash your pet, use Hammer 3 and Hammer 5, then go back to Unleash Ranger. Make sure that your pet skills are on autocast. The way you set them to autocast is you hold Control and right-click them. If you end up unleashing your pet, those skills do not autocast, so make sure that once you go for a break bar you go back to Unleash Ranger, because otherwise it will be a pretty major DPS loss. The way you can tell what mode you're in is that you should have the Unleash Pet icon, your pet skills should be on autocast, and all of your Hammer skills should be green.

Ranger doesn't have a whole lot of notable utility. Healing Spring is a decent condi cleanse for your whole team. For your elite, I'd probably recommend Entangle. This is basically a really long immobilize and can be useful in fights like Gorseval split phases. Exploding Spores is a little bit of CC. Protect Me! is a group stun break. Search and Rescue! will revive a downed ally. Most ranger skills are kind of meh and pretty selfish. Technically you could run Stone Spirit, but it's probably very conditional.

That's it. I hope you all have success with the build. Thank you.

items.title: 5 Button Druid Condi 35k
items.rotation: Set your pet skill on auto cast (ctrl right click).
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity ranger build for fractal/raid/strike content.

You can drop it to 3 buttons if you drop dagger 3/celestial avatar 3 and sharpening stone to be around 30k. Your 2, 5 skills are important.

Also for LI reasons I don't like https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Light_on_your_Feet. I don't like when these builds have forced movement, especially dodges that I assume players playing these builds will need.

0:00 Rotation
2:15 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, you want to precast your Sharpening Stone. Your opener will be Torch 5 into Dagger 2 into Dagger 3 into your Celestial Avatar combo. Your Celestial Avatar combo will always be the same: Celestial Avatar 5 into two Celestial Avatar 2s into one Celestial Avatar 3. This build takes Quick Draw, which procs whenever you enter Celestial Avatar. You can see how I got two casts of Celestial Avatar 2 during Celestial Avatar because you can cast Celestial Avatar 2 while you're in Celestial Avatar 5.

For your rotation in general, you will always want to use Dagger 2 whenever it's up into Dagger 3 or Torch 5 whenever they're up. Dagger 3 and Torch 5 are kind of the same on the priority order, so you can pretty much just do 2 into 3 into 5, or 2 into 5, or 2 into 3 when you exit Celestial Avatar. You will want to exit Celestial Avatar whenever you are done with the Celestial Avatar combo. You'll want to continuously cast Sharpening Stone whenever it's up. One thing I should mention about Celestial Avatar is that skill 3 is a CC. Since we take Quick Draw, you can alter the combo a little bit to get a little more CC out of it. If the boss has a break bar, instead of doing 5 into two 2s into 3, you can do 3 into 5 into 2 into 3.

For your pets, you want to make sure that you're running either the Spider or the Devourer, but it shouldn't matter too much. It's a pretty minimal DPS difference. Dagger 3 is a mobility skill. For your healing skill, you can take Healing Spring. This is a condi cleanse for you and your team. For your elite, you can pretty much just always take Entangle. There are some uses for Glyph of Stars because it's another condi cleanse and stability skill, but you can probably just take Entangle and squeeze out a little more damage if you wish. If you're going to replace any utility, you should probably replace Signet of the Hunt. Some useful skills are Glyph of Tides for a little CC and a push, Protect Me! to break you and your allies out of stun, Search and Rescue! to send your pet to revive an ally, Spike Trap if you want a little more CC, Viper's Nest if you want a little more damage, and even Sun Spirit if you want more damage than Signet of the Wild. I think that's it for the build rundown. I hope you all enjoy the build. Thank you.
